title: TSMC being investigated by USITC for alleged patent violations
gist: A reposted Taipei Times report: the USITC is investigating TSMC over a patent-infringement complaint by two Irish IPValue/Vector Capital subsidiaries (Longitude Licensing, Marlin Semiconductor); Taiwan's IP office head calls them patent trolls, TSMC is challenging patent validity at the PTAB, and a preliminary ruling is expected this month with a final decision possible in October.

insights.claim: TSMC faces a USITC investigation triggered by a complaint from Longitude Licensing and Marlin Semiconductor accusing it of using their patented technology to produce chips, with a preliminary administrative-law-judge ruling expected in June 2026 and a possible final USITC decision in October 2026.
insights.speaker: Fred Chen
insights.so_what: An adverse ITC ruling could threaten import of TSMC-made chips into the US; the October decision is a discrete event-risk date for TSMC and its US fabless customers.
insights.quote: An administrative law judge is expected to issue a preliminary ruling this month, with the USITC possibly making a final decision in October, the report said.

insights.claim: Four Republican lawmakers have urged the USITC to block imports of foreign-made chips found to infringe US patents in the TSMC case, raising the political stakes of the ruling.
insights.speaker: Fred Chen
insights.so_what: Political pressure to enforce import bans increases tail-risk of a disruptive remedy; watch whether the ITC leans toward exclusion orders.
insights.quote: four Republican lawmakers have urged the USITC to block imports of foreign-made chips found to infringe US patents in the case involving TSMC

insights.claim: Taiwan IP Office Director-General Liao Cheng-wei publicly characterized the two Irish plaintiffs as patent trolls (non-practicing entities) seeking licensing fees or a settlement rather than manufacturing products.
insights.speaker: Fred Chen
insights.so_what: Framing the suit as troll litigation signals Taiwan official backing for TSMC and suggests a settlement-or-invalidation path rather than a substantive product threat.
insights.quote: he suspected that two firms suing contract chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC, 台積電) for patent infringement are “patent trolls.”

insights.claim: The two Irish plaintiffs are subsidiaries of IPValue Management, owned by San Francisco private-equity firm Vector Capital, and Marlin Semiconductor acquired part of its patents from Taiwan's UMC in 2021.
insights.speaker: Fred Chen
insights.so_what: The patents originate partly from rival UMC, framing the dispute as competitor-derived IP monetized via PE; relevant to UMC and IP-licensing dynamics.
insights.quote: Marlin Semiconductor, in particular, acquired a portion of the patents from United Microelectronics Corp (聯電), a smaller contract chipmaker in Taiwan, in 2021.
insights.locator: The two Irish companies are subsidiaries of IPValue Management...

insights.claim: TSMC has filed a PTAB petition arguing the disputed patents are invalid for lack of novelty or inventiveness because they were already published, which if accepted would force the plaintiffs to withdraw.
insights.speaker: Fred Chen
insights.so_what: TSMC's invalidity defense is the key swing factor; a PTAB win would neutralize the ITC threat, so PTAB outcome is the leading indicator to track.
insights.quote: TSMC has filed a petition with the US Patent and Trademark Office’s Patent Trial and Appeal Board to challenge the firms’ allegations, saying the patents in question are invalid
